author: Dennis
msg:
[Photo] Good morning everyone,

I want to give a clear overview of my current situation and findings.

To better understand how calibration affects rewards relative to the number of phones per IP, I reduced my setup to one phone per IP. After that, I gradually brought devices back online, adding one phone per day, until I reached a maximum of five phones on a single IP.

From the data, you can clearly see the impact of both the reduction and the gradual reintroduction of devices. The pattern is consistent:

After adding a device, there is a spike in rewards.
This is followed by a steady decline over the next days.

This behavior is especially visible starting around the 6th, where the newly added ULO shows the same pattern: initial peak, then degradation. Even with only up to five phones on a single IP, rewards drop from strong levels down to around 0.08.

Based on this, the commonly referenced “20 devices per IP” rule does not appear to hold true in the current state of the system.

author: Dennis
msg:
[Photo] I understand the broader context. There are clearly issues with large-scale synthetic setups, which likely affect client trust and willingness to pay. This is not a trivial problem—even major companies like Google or Microsoft struggle to fully prevent such abuse.

However, the key question is whether Unity can address these bad actors without negatively impacting smaller, legitimate setups like ours. If enforcing limits (e.g., 20 devices per IP) were straightforward, it raises the question of why it hasn’t been implemented clearly and effectively.

I’m not a developer, but it seems likely that deeper structural changes are required rather than simple rule enforcement.

At this point, it also feels like some client trust may have been weakened.

To be realistic: it might currently be more reasonable to step away from the project temporarily, turn off devices, and communicate that decision to your ULOs as well. The growing frustration within the community may be doing more harm than a short pause.

author: Dennis
msg: I understand this is easier for me to say, as my setup is relatively small compared to others.

author: Gwims
msg: Josh said there was no limits of devices to one IP, 20 was just what he recommended in past AMAs  that was based on health concerns if you listened to all the AMAs at least that’s the feeling I got my opinion based what what I listened to. we have only one task at the moment that’s active for this specific task. I feel this is fluid and changing companies that pay for this data/information it has to be valuable to them. If it’s repeated same data 30 40 times probably not relevant after awhile. The synthetic attack on rewards didn’t make it any better I imagine. I believe this tool will help a lot lol to stop bad actors in future and lets us see the value our devices provide to the network. As much as I’m feeling the crunch  it makes sense that if the system isnt providing the value then you get paid according to the value that generated for the buyer. Hopefully once this foundation is set we don’t get too many more hiccups in future  or at least with multiple tasks on the table we won’t really feel it

author: Flippy
msg:
The situation hasn't changed since our last update — the recalculation and bad actor cleanup are still ongoing across the network. This affects all operators, not
  just your setup.

  We don't have new information to share at this point, and we're not able to schedule calls from the support side. Once the recalculation is complete, it will be
  communicated through the official channels (Telegram, Discord, X). Legitimate operators should see rewards stabilise after that.

  Kind regards,
  Unity Nodes Support Team

author: L .
msg:
All iPhone 7s on wifi stay connected. 19 at 2 different locations. 
Loc 1: 97-98% uptime last 30 days
Loc 2: 85(3phones)-98% uptime last 7 days(new location)

1 iPhone 11 on wifi that disconnects randomly. 46.5% uptime last 30 days.  Just have to refresh the app & it connects.

iPhone 17(personal phone) 88.1% uptime last 30 days.

All phones are optimized through settings to let the app run.

Never had to force quit & restart.  Aside from iPhone 11, all phones show as connected when I physically look at them.
